label: 1040_bank policy_policy transmission_10185941 monetary_policy rate
description: This work examines how monetary policy affects credit and the real economy, especially in developing nations where financial systems are underdeveloped. It highlights the challenges faced by these countries in implementing effective monetary policies due to weak institutions and limited market depth.

central_articles.title: Monetary policy, credit dynamics, and economic activity in developing countries
central_articles.excerpt: The ability of monetary policy to influence credit and the real economy is of central concern to policymakers and academics. central_articles.title: Monetary Policy Transmission in the GCC Countries
central_articles.excerpt: Summary The GCC countries maintain a policy of open capital accounts and a pegged (or nearly-pegged) exchange rate, thereby reducing their freedom to run an independent monetary policy. This paper shows, however, that the pass-through of policy rates to retail rates is on the low side, reflecting the shallowness of…
